steps.title: Isolate the absolute value
steps.content:
Start with $-4|6x+5|+8=-12$. Subtract 8 from both sides to isolate the absolute value term:
$$-4|6x+5|=-20$$
Divide both sides by $-4$:
$$|6x+5|=5$$

steps.title: Split into two cases
steps.content:
An absolute value equation $|A|=5$ means the expression inside equals $5$ or $-5$. Set up both cases:
  • Case 1: $6x+5=5$
  • Case 2: $6x+5=-5$

steps.title: Solve both cases
steps.content:
Case 1: $6x+5=5$
$$6x=0$$
$$x=0$$
Case 2: $6x+5=-5$
$$6x=-10$$
$$x=-\frac{5}{3}$$
The two solutions are $x=0$ and $x=-\frac{5}{3}$.

C is correct.
